# OpenBin

> OpenBin is an open source inventory management system that makes physical storage searchable. Create bins for your stuff, let AI identify contents from a photo, and print QR labels to find anything with a scan.

## Key Facts

- License: AGPL-3.0 (open source)
- Self-hosted: One Docker container, runs on a Raspberry Pi
- Cloud: Free tier available, Plus at $3/mo, Pro at $6/mo
- AI providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini (bring your own key for self-hosted)
- Multi-user: Invite codes, three roles (admin, member, viewer)
- Data export: CSV and JSON, one-click export
- API: Full REST API + MCP server for AI assistant integration

## Core Features

- QR label generation and scanning
- AI photo recognition for inventory cataloging
- Natural language inventory commands ("move all batteries to the garage")
- AI-powered reorganization suggestions
- Bulk operations and bulk add
- Search and filter across all bins, items, and tags
- Dashboard with activity feed and quick actions
- Custom fields and saved views

- Homepage: https://openbin.app

- Docker Quick Start: https://openbin.app/docs/getting-started/docker
- AI Features: https://openbin.app/docs/guide/ai
- API Reference: https://openbin.app/docs/api/
- FAQ: https://openbin.app/docs/guide/faq
- GitHub: https://github.com/akifbayram/openbin
